member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode
member.summary: Return codes for mt32emu operations.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.Ok
member.summary: Operation completed normally.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.AddedControlRom
member.summary: A full control ROM was successfully added.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.AddedPcmRom
member.summary: A full PCM ROM was successfully added.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.AddedPartialControlRom
member.summary: A partial control ROM was successfully added.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.AddedPartialPcmRom
member.summary: A partial PCM ROM was successfully added.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.RomNotIdentified
member.summary: The ROM data could not be identified.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.FileNotFound
member.summary: The specified file was not found.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.FileNotLoaded
member.summary: The file could not be loaded.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.MissingRoms
member.summary: Required ROMs are missing.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.NotOpened
member.summary: The synth has not been opened.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.QueueFull
member.summary: The MIDI event queue is full.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.RomsNotPairable
member.summary: The ROM images are not pairable.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.MachineNotIdentified
member.summary: The specified machine could not be identified.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReturnCode.Failed
member.summary: An undefined error occurred.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean
member.summary: Boolean type used by the mt32emu C API.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean.False
member.summary: False.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean.True
member.summary: True.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacInputMode
member.summary: Methods for emulating the connection between LA32 and the DAC, which involves semi-declicking applied to the weights of individual partials mixed at the DAC.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacInputMode.Nice
member.summary: Produces output samples in the nice way found by ROKr (Ukraine declicker), all declicking is performed by the GT/declicker code.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacInputMode.Pure
member.summary: Roles of GT and declicker are GT-declicked (declicking is performed by GT only), note that GT still has a minor effect on declicking.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacInputMode.Generation1
member.summary: GT is generating samples as close to the real hardware as possible, but declicker is applied. This is the closest mode to the real hardware.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacInputMode.Generation2
member.summary: GT is generating samples as close to the real CM-32L / LAPC-I as possible, but declicker is applied.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiDelayMode
member.summary: Methods for emulating the MIDI interface delay.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiDelayMode.Immediate
member.summary: All received MIDI messages are processed immediately.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiDelayMode.DelayShortMessagesOnly
member.summary: Only short MIDI messages are delayed as if they were transferred via a serial MIDI interface.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiDelayMode.DelayAll
member.summary: All received MIDI messages are delayed as if they were transferred via a serial MIDI interface.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuAnalogOutputMode
member.summary: Methods for emulating the effects of analogue circuits on the output signal.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuAnalogOutputMode.DigitalOnly
member.summary: Only digital path is emulated. The output samples correspond to the digital signal at the DAC entrance. Fastest mode.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuAnalogOutputMode.Coarse
member.summary: Roles of the coarse emulation of analogue circuits. Fast, produces passable output.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuAnalogOutputMode.Accurate
member.summary: Roles of more accurate emulation of analogue circuits. Slower but more accurate.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuAnalogOutputMode.Oversampled
member.summary: Uses oversampling for the most accurate emulation. Slowest but highest quality.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuPartialState
member.summary: States of a partial in the synthesizer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuPartialState.Inactive
member.summary: The partial is inactive and available for use.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuPartialState.Attack
member.summary: The partial is in the attack phase.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuPartialState.Sustain
member.summary: The partial is in the sustain phase.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuPartialState.Release
member.summary: The partial is in the release phase.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuSamplerateConversionQuality
member.summary: Quality options for sample rate conversion. All options except guarantee full suppression of aliasing noise for 16-bit integer samples.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuSamplerateConversionQuality.Fastest
member.summary: Fastest conversion with potential aliasing artifacts.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuSamplerateConversionQuality.Fast
member.summary: Fast conversion with good quality.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuSamplerateConversionQuality.Good
member.summary: Good quality conversion.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuSamplerateConversionQuality.Best
member.summary: Best quality conversion. Slowest.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRendererType
member.summary: Type of the wave generator and renderer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRendererType.Bit16S
member.summary: Use 16-bit signed integer renderer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRendererType.Float
member.summary: Use 32-bit floating point renderer.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReportHandlerVersion
member.summary: Report handler interface versions.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReportHandlerVersion.Version0
member.summary: Version 0 of the report handler interface.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReportHandlerVersion.Version1
member.summary: Version 1 of the report handler interface.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReportHandlerVersion.Version2
member.summary: Version 2 of the report handler interface.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReportHandlerVersion.Current
member.summary: The current version of the report handler interface.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiReceiverVersion
member.summary: MIDI receiver interface versions.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiReceiverVersion.Version0
member.summary: Version 0 of the MIDI receiver interface.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiReceiverVersion.Current
member.summary: The current version of the MIDI receiver interface.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative
member.summary: P/Invoke bindings for the mt32emu C library — a synthesiser emulation of the Roland MT-32, CM-32L, and LAPC-I modules. All methods map directly to the corresponding C functions declared in c_interface.h.

remarks.para: The native library is resolved automatically based on the runtime identifier (RID) of the current platform. Supported platforms: Windows (x86, x64, arm64), Linux (x64, arm64), macOS (x64, arm64).
On Windows, the calling convention is __cdecl. On other platforms, the default calling convention applies.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.LibraryName
member.summary: The name of the native mt32emu shared library. Resolved via the .NET runtime's native library probing.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetSupportedReportHandlerVersion
member.summary: Returns the version ID of the report handler interface the library was compiled with. This allows a client to fall back gracefully instead of silently not receiving expected event reports.
member.returns: The supported report handler version.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetSupportedMidiReceiverVersion
member.summary: Returns the version ID of the MIDI receiver interface the library was compiled with. This allows a client to fall back gracefully instead of silently not receiving expected MIDI messages.
member.returns: The supported MIDI receiver version.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetLibraryVersionInt
member.summary: Returns the library version as an integer in format: 0x00MMmmpp, where MM is the major version, mm is the minor version, and pp is the patch number.
member.returns: The library version encoded as a 32-bit unsigned integer.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetLibraryVersionStringPtr
member.summary: Returns the library version as a null-terminated C string in format: "MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH".
member.returns: A pointer to a null-terminated string containing the version.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetLibraryVersionString
member.summary: Returns the library version as a managed string in format: "MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH".
member.returns: The library version string.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetStereoOutputSamplerate(Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuAnalogOutputMode)
member.summary: Returns the output sample rate used in emulation of stereo analog circuitry of hardware units for a particular .
member.param: The analog output mode to query the sample rate for.
member.returns: The output sample rate in Hz for the specified mode.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetBestAnalogOutputMode(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the for which the output signal may retain its full frequency spectrum at the sample rate specified by .
member.returns: The best analog output mode for the specified sample rate.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetMachineIds(System.IntPtr,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Retrieves a list of identifiers (as C strings) of supported machines.
member.param: Pointer to an array of to be filled with string pointers. Pass to query the count.
The size of the array. Ignored when is .
member.returns: The number of machine identifiers available. When is , returns the required array size.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetRomIds(System.IntPtr,System.UIntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Retrieves a list of identifiers (as C strings) of supported ROM images.
member.param: Pointer to an array of to be filled with string pointers. Pass to query the count.
The size of the array. Ignored when is .
Optional machine identifier to filter ROM images. Pass to retrieve all ROM identifiers.
member.returns: The number of ROM identifiers available. Returns 0 if is unrecognised.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IdentifyRomData(Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo@,System.IntPtr,System.UIntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Identifies a ROM image contained in the provided data array by its SHA1 digest.
member.param: A structure to be filled with identified ROM details. Unused fields are set to .
Pointer to the ROM data array.
Size of the array in bytes.
Optional machine identifier. Pass for any machine.
member.returns: upon success or a negative error code.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IdentifyRomFile(Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo@,System.String,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Loads the content of the file specified by and identifies the ROM image it contains by its SHA1 digest.
member.param: A structure to be filled with identified ROM details. Unused fields are set to .
Optional machine identifier. Pass for any machine.
member.returns: upon success or a negative error code.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.CreateContext(Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReportHandlerI,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Initialises a new emulation context and optionally installs a custom report handler.
member.param: Report handler interface for receiving callbacks. Pass default if no report handler is needed.
User-supplied instance data pointer passed to report handler callbacks.
member.returns: An opaque handle to the newly created emulation context.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.FreeContext(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Closes and destroys the emulation context, freeing all associated resources.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.AddRomData(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UIntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Adds a new full ROM data image identified by its SHA1 digest to the emulation context, replacing a previously added ROM of the same type if any.
member.param: Pointer to the ROM data array. The array is NOT copied and used directly for efficiency. The caller must keep it alive while the context refers to it.
Size of the array in bytes.
Pointer to a 41-byte SHA1 digest string. Pass to have it computed from the data.
member.returns: A positive value upon success.
member.remarks: This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. Newly added ROM will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.AddRomFile(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: Loads a ROM file containing a full ROM data image, identifies it by SHA1 digest, and adds it to the emulation context.
member.returns: A positive value upon success.
member.remarks: This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. Newly added ROM will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.MergeAndAddRomData(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UIntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UIntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Merges a pair of compatible ROM data image parts into a full image and adds it to the emulation context.
member.param: Pointer to the first partial ROM data.
SHA1 digest of part 1, or to compute from data.
Pointer to the second partial ROM data.
SHA1 digest of part 2, or to compute from data.
member.returns: A positive value upon success.
member.remarks: The provided data arrays may be deallocated as soon as this function completes. This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. Newly added ROM will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.MergeAndAddRomFiles(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Loads a pair of files containing compatible parts of a full ROM image, merges them, and adds the result to the emulation context.
member.param: Path to the first partial ROM file.
Path to the second partial ROM file.
member.returns: A positive value upon success.
member.remarks: This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. Newly added ROM will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.AddMachineRomFile(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Loads a file containing a ROM image compatible with the specified machine, identifies it, and adds it to the emulation context following specific merging rules for partial images.
member.returns: A positive value if changes were made, if the file was ignored, or a negative error code upon failure.

remarks.para: Full and partial ROM images are supported. The described behaviour allows traversing a directory of ROM files, adding each one in turn until both control and PCM ROMs are complete.
This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. Newly added ROMs will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetRomInfo(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo@)
member.summary: Fills in a structure with identifiers and descriptions of the control and PCM ROM files currently loaded in the context.
member.param: A structure to be filled. Unloaded ROM fields are set to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetPartialCount(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Overrides the default maximum number of partials playing simultaneously.
member.remarks: This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. The new value will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetAnalogOutputMode(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuAnalogOutputMode)
member.summary: Overrides the default analog output mode for the emulation session.
member.remarks: This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. The new value will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetStereoOutputSamplerate(System.IntPtr,System.Double)
member.summary: Sets the desired output sample rate for the synthesiser. When set to 0, the default sample rate (dependent on the analog output mode) is used.
member.param: The desired output sample rate in Hz, or 0 for the default.
member.remarks: This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. The new value will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetSamplerateConversionQuality(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuSamplerateConversionQuality)
member.summary: Sets the sample rate conversion quality, trading off speed vs. retained passband width.
member.remarks: This function does not immediately change the state of an already opened synth. The new value will take effect upon the next call to .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SelectRendererType(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRendererType)
member.summary: Selects the type of wave generator and renderer to use in subsequent calls to . By default, is selected.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetSelectedRendererType(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the previously selected renderer type.
member.returns: The currently selected renderer type.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.OpenSynth(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Prepares the emulation context to receive MIDI messages and produce audio output using the previously added ROMs.
member.returns: upon success.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.CloseSynth(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Closes the synth, freeing allocated resources. Added ROMs remain unaffected and ready for reuse.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsOpen(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether the synth is in a completely initialized state.
member.returns: if the synth is open, otherwise .

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetActualStereoOutputSamplerate(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the actual output sample rate of the fully processed stereo signal.
member.returns: The actual output sample rate in Hz.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.ConvertOutputToSynthTimestamp(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Converts a timestamp from output sample rate to internal synth sample rate (32000 Hz).
member.returns: Equivalent timestamp in synth samples (32000 Hz).

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.ConvertSynthToOutputTimestamp(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Converts a timestamp from internal synth sample rate (32000 Hz) to output sample rate.
member.returns: Equivalent timestamp in output samples.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.FlushMidiQueue(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Processes all enqueued MIDI events immediately.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetMidiEventQueueSize(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Sets the size of the internal MIDI event queue. The actual size is rounded up to the nearest power of 2. The queue is flushed before reallocation.
member.returns: The actual queue size being used.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.ConfigureMidiEventQueueSysexStorage(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Configures the SysEx storage of the internal MIDI event queue.
member.param: When 0, SysEx data is stored in dynamically allocated buffers (default, not realtime-safe). When positive, a single preallocated buffer of the specified size is used (realtime-safe).
member.remarks: The queue is flushed and recreated so its size remains intact.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetMidiReceiver(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiReceiverI,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Installs a custom MIDI receiver for receiving MIDI messages generated by the stream parser.
member.param: The MIDI receiver interface. Pass default to restore default behaviour.
User-supplied instance data pointer passed to MIDI receiver callbacks.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetInternalRenderedSampleCount(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the current value of the global counter of samples rendered since the synth was created (at native 32000 Hz). Useful for computing accurate MIDI message timestamps.
member.returns: The total number of samples rendered at 32000 Hz.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.ParseStream(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Parses a block of raw MIDI bytes and enqueues parsed messages for immediate processing. SysEx messages may be fragmented across calls. Running status is handled for short messages.
member.param: Pointer to the raw MIDI byte stream.
Length of the stream in bytes.
member.remarks: The total length of a fragmented SysEx message must not exceed 32768 bytes.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.ParseStreamAt(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Parses a block of raw MIDI bytes and enqueues parsed messages to play at the specified time.
member.param: Pointer to the raw MIDI byte stream.
Length of the stream in bytes.
Playback timestamp in synth samples (32000 Hz).

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlayShortMessage(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Enqueues a single 32-bit encoded short MIDI message for immediate processing. Running status is used if no status byte is present.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlayShortMessageAt(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Enqueues a single 32-bit encoded short MIDI message to play at the specified time.
member.param: Playback timestamp in synth samples (32000 Hz).

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlayMsg(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Enqueues a single short MIDI message for immediate processing. The message must contain a status byte.
member.param: The 32-bit encoded MIDI message with status byte.
member.returns: upon success or a negative error code.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlaySysex(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Enqueues a single well-formed System Exclusive MIDI message for immediate processing.
member.param: Pointer to the SysEx data buffer.
Length of the SysEx data in bytes.
member.returns: upon success or a negative error code.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlayMsgAt(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Enqueues a single short MIDI message to play at the specified time. The message must contain a status byte.
member.param: The 32-bit encoded MIDI message with status byte.
Playback timestamp in synth samples (32000 Hz).
member.returns: upon success or a negative error code.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlaySysexAt(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Enqueues a single well-formed System Exclusive MIDI message to play at the specified time.
member.param: Pointer to the SysEx data buffer.
Length of the SysEx data in bytes.
Playback timestamp in synth samples (32000 Hz).
member.returns: upon success or a negative error code.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlayMsgNow(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Sends a short MIDI message to the synth for immediate playback. The message must contain a status byte and two data bytes.

remarks.para: WARNING: This method may have no effect while the synth is aborting a poly. It does not ensure minimum 1-sample delay between sequential MIDI events.
The calling thread must be synchronised with the rendering thread or be the same thread.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlayMsgOnPart(System.IntPtr,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Sends an unpacked short MIDI message to the synth for immediate playback.
member.param: Part number: 0–7 for Part 1–8, or 8 for Rhythm.
MIDI command (8–14), representing the high 4 bits of the status byte.

remarks.para: WARNING: This method may have no effect while the synth is aborting a poly.
The calling thread must be synchronised with the rendering thread or be the same thread.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PlaySysexNow(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Sends a single well-formed System Exclusive MIDI message for immediate processing.
member.param: Pointer to the SysEx data buffer.
Length of the SysEx data in bytes.

remarks.para: WARNING: This method may have no effect while the synth is aborting a poly.
The calling thread must be synchronised with the rendering thread or be the same thread.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.WriteSysex(System.IntPtr,System.Byte,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Sends the inner body of a System Exclusive MIDI message for direct processing.
member.param: Pointer to the SysEx body data buffer.
Length of the SysEx body in bytes.

remarks.para: WARNING: This method may have no effect while the synth is aborting a poly.
The calling thread must be synchronised with the rendering thread or be the same thread.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetReverbEnabled(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Enables or disables wet reverb output.
member.param: Whether reverb output should be enabled.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsReverbEnabled(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether wet reverb output is enabled.
member.returns: if reverb is enabled.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetReverbOverridden(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Sets override reverb mode. When active, sysexes controlling reverb parameters are ignored.
member.param: Whether reverb settings should be overridden.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsReverbOverridden(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether reverb settings are currently overridden.
member.returns: if reverb is overridden.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetReverbCompatibilityMode(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Forces reverb model compatibility mode.
member.param: When , forces old MT-32 reverb circuit emulation. When , forces new generation (CM-32L/LAPC-I) reverb.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsMt32ReverbCompatibilityMode(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether reverb is in old MT-32 compatibility mode.
member.returns: if using old MT-32 compatible reverb.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsDefaultReverbMt32Compatible(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether the default reverb compatibility mode is the old MT-32 compatible mode.
member.returns: if the default reverb is MT-32 compatible.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.PreallocateReverbMemory(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Controls whether reverb buffers for all modes are preallocated to avoid memory operations on the rendering thread.
member.param: When , all reverb buffers are kept allocated. When (default), unused buffers are freed to save memory.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetDacInputMode(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacInputMode)
member.summary: Sets the DAC input mode.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetDacInputMode(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the current DAC input mode.
member.returns: The current DAC input mode.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetMidiDelayMode(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiDelayMode)
member.summary: Sets the MIDI delay mode.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetMidiDelayMode(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the current MIDI delay mode.
member.returns: The current MIDI delay mode.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetOutputGain(System.IntPtr,System.Single)
member.summary: Sets the output gain factor for synth output channels. This is applied to all output samples and corresponds to the gain of the output analog circuitry, independent of the synth's Master volume.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetOutputGain(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the current output gain factor for synth output channels.
member.returns: The current output gain.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetReverbOutputGain(System.IntPtr,System.Single)
member.summary: Sets the output gain factor for the reverb wet output channels. Together with , allows independent control of reverb and non-reverb channels.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetReverbOutputGain(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the current output gain factor for reverb wet output channels.
member.returns: The current reverb output gain.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetMasterVolumeOverride(System.IntPtr,System.Byte)
member.summary: Sets or removes an override for the Master Volume. When overridden, SysEx writes to the system area have no effect on the Master Volume, yet system memory reads return the overridden value.
member.param: A value in range 0–100 to enable override, or a value outside this range to disable it.
member.remarks: This setting persists across synth reopening.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetMasterVolumeOverride(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the overridden master volume, if any. A value outside 0–100 means no override is in effect.
member.returns: The overridden master volume or a value >100 if no override is active.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetPartVolumeOverride(System.IntPtr,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Sets or removes an override for the output level on a specific part. When overridden, MIDI Volume (CC 7) and SysEx Patch temp writes are disregarded for this part.
member.param: Part number: 0–7 for Part 1–8, or 8 for Rhythm.
A value in range 0–100 to enable override, or a value outside this range to disable it.
member.remarks: Setting to 0 completely mutes the part (unlike real hardware behaviour).

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetPartVolumeOverride(System.IntPtr,System.Byte)
member.summary: Returns the overridden volume for a specific part. A value outside 0–100 means no override is in effect.
member.param: Part number: 0–7 for Part 1–8, or 8 for Rhythm.
member.returns: The overridden volume or a value >100 if no override is active.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetReversedStereoEnabled(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Swaps left and right output channels.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsReversedStereoEnabled(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether left and right output channels are swapped.
member.returns: if stereo is reversed.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetNiceAmpRampEnabled(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Toggles NiceAmpRamp mode, which ensures amp ramp never jumps to the target value. Enabled by default for quality improvement.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsNiceAmpRampEnabled(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether NiceAmpRamp mode is enabled.
member.returns: if NiceAmpRamp is enabled.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetNicePanningEnabled(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Toggles NicePanning mode, which enlarges pan setting accuracy from 3 bits to 4 bits. Disabled by default.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsNicePanningEnabled(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether NicePanning mode is enabled.
member.returns: if NicePanning is enabled.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetNicePartialMixingEnabled(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Toggles NicePartialMixing mode, which ensures partials are always mixed in-phase. Disabled by default.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsNicePartialMixingEnabled(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether NicePartialMixing mode is enabled.
member.returns: if NicePartialMixing is enabled.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.RenderBit16s(System.IntPtr,System.Int16[],System.UInt32)
member.summary: Renders audio samples to the specified output buffer as 16-bit signed integer stereo.
member.param: Output buffer for interleaved stereo samples. Must hold at least × 2 elements.
Number of stereo frames to render (not bytes; one frame = 2 samples = 4 bytes).

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.RenderFloat(System.IntPtr,System.Single[],System.UInt32)
member.summary: Renders audio samples to the specified output buffer as 32-bit floating point stereo.
member.param: Output buffer for interleaved stereo float samples. Must hold at least × 2 elements.
Number of stereo frames to render.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.RenderBit16sStreams(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputBit16sStreams@,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Renders audio samples to the specified multiplexed 16-bit signed integer output streams at the DAC entrance. No analog circuitry emulation is applied.
member.param: The DAC output stream pointers. Any stream pointer may be to skip it.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.RenderFloatStreams(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputFloatStreams@,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Renders audio samples to the specified multiplexed float output streams at the DAC entrance. No analog circuitry emulation is applied.
member.param: The DAC output stream pointers. Any stream pointer may be to skip it.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.HasActivePartials(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether there is at least one active partial.
member.returns: if any partial is active.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsActive(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether the synth is considered active (has active partials or reverb is detected as active).
member.returns: if the synth is active.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetPartialCount(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the maximum number of partials that can play simultaneously.
member.returns: The maximum partial count.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetPartStates(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns current states of all 9 parts as a bit set. The least significant bit corresponds to Part 1. A set bit indicates at least one active non-releasing partial on the part.
member.returns: A 32-bit value with the lower 9 bits representing part states.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetPartialStates(System.IntPtr,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Fills in current states of all partials. Each byte holds states of 4 partials (2 bits each), starting from the least significant bits.
member.param: Array to receive partial states. Must be large enough to hold all partials.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetPlayingNotes(System.IntPtr,System.Byte,System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Fills in information about currently playing notes on the specified part.
member.param: Part number: 0–7 for Part 1–8, or 8 for Rhythm.
Array to receive MIDI key numbers of playing notes.
Array to receive velocities of playing notes.
member.returns: The number of currently playing notes on the specified part.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetPatchNamePtr(System.IntPtr,System.Byte)
member.summary: Returns the name of the patch set on the specified part as a C string pointer.
member.param: Part number: 0–7 for Part 1–8, or 8 for Rhythm.
member.returns: Pointer to a null-terminated string. Valid until the next rendering or immediate SysEx processing call.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetPatchName(System.IntPtr,System.Byte)
member.summary: Returns the name of the patch set on the specified part as a managed string.
member.param: Part number: 0–7 for Part 1–8, or 8 for Rhythm.
member.returns: The patch name, or an empty string if unavailable.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetSoundGroupName(System.IntPtr,System.Byte[],System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Retrieves the name of the sound group associated with the specified timbre.
member.param: A byte array of at least 8 elements to receive the null-terminated sound group name.
Timbre group: 0 = GROUP A, 1 = GROUP B, 2 = MEMORY, 3 = RHYTHM.
Timbre number: 0–63 for banks other than RHYTHM.
member.returns: if the timbre was found and the name written.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetSoundName(System.IntPtr,System.Byte[],System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Retrieves the name of the timbre identified by group and number.
member.param: A byte array of at least 11 elements to receive the null-terminated timbre name.
Timbre group: 0 = GROUP A, 1 = GROUP B, 2 = MEMORY, 3 = RHYTHM.
Timbre number: 0–63 for banks other than RHYTHM.
member.returns: if the timbre was found and the name written.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.ReadMemory(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.UInt32,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Stores internal state of the emulated synth into the provided array (as it would be acquired from hardware).
member.param: The memory address to read from.
Array to receive the memory contents.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.GetDisplayState(System.IntPtr,System.Byte[],Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Retrieves the current state of the emulated MT-32 display.
member.param: A byte array of at least 21 elements to receive the null-terminated display string.
When , enables condensed 16-character representation for narrow LCDs.
member.returns: if the MIDI MESSAGE LED is ON.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetMainDisplayMode(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Resets the emulated LCD to the main mode (Master Volume), equivalent to pressing the Master Volume button.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.SetDisplayCompatibility(System.IntPtr,Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuBoolean)
member.summary: Selects an arbitrary display emulation model independent of the control ROM version.
member.param: When , forces old-gen MT-32 display behaviour. When , forces new-gen (CM-32L/LAPC-I) display behaviour.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsDisplayOldMt32Compatible(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether the currently configured display features are compatible with old-gen MT-32 devices.
member.returns: if old MT-32 display compatibility is active.

member.name: M:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuNative.IsDefaultDisplayOldMt32Compatible(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns whether the default display features (based on ROM version) are compatible with old-gen MT-32 devices.
member.returns: if the default display is old MT-32 compatible.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo
member.summary: Contains identifiers and descriptions of ROM files being used by the emulation context.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo.ControlRomId
member.summary: Identifier of the control ROM.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo.ControlRomDescription
member.summary: Description of the control ROM.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo.ControlRomSha1Digest
member.summary: SHA1 digest of the control ROM.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo.PcmRomId
member.summary: Identifier of the PCM ROM.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo.PcmRomDescription
member.summary: Description of the PCM ROM.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuRomInfo.PcmRomSha1Digest
member.summary: SHA1 digest of the PCM ROM.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputBit16sStreams
member.summary: Set of multiplexed output 16-bit signed integer streams appeared at the DAC entrance.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputBit16sStreams.NonReverbLeft
member.summary: Pointer to the non-reverb left channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputBit16sStreams.NonReverbRight
member.summary: Pointer to the non-reverb right channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputBit16sStreams.ReverbDryLeft
member.summary: Pointer to the reverb dry left channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputBit16sStreams.ReverbDryRight
member.summary: Pointer to the reverb dry right channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputBit16sStreams.ReverbWetLeft
member.summary: Pointer to the reverb wet left channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputBit16sStreams.ReverbWetRight
member.summary: Pointer to the reverb wet right channel output buffer.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputFloatStreams
member.summary: Set of multiplexed output float streams appeared at the DAC entrance.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputFloatStreams.NonReverbLeft
member.summary: Pointer to the non-reverb left channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputFloatStreams.NonReverbRight
member.summary: Pointer to the non-reverb right channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputFloatStreams.ReverbDryLeft
member.summary: Pointer to the reverb dry left channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputFloatStreams.ReverbDryRight
member.summary: Pointer to the reverb dry right channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputFloatStreams.ReverbWetLeft
member.summary: Pointer to the reverb wet left channel output buffer.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuDacOutputFloatStreams.ReverbWetRight
member.summary: Pointer to the reverb wet right channel output buffer.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReportHandlerI
member.summary: Report handler interface union. Passed to to receive event callbacks. This is an opaque pointer type — use if no report handler is needed.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuReportHandlerI.Pointer
member.summary: Pointer to the report handler interface vtable.

member.name: T:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiReceiverI
member.summary: MIDI receiver interface union. Passed to to receive parsed MIDI messages. This is an opaque pointer type — use if no MIDI receiver is needed.

member.name: F:Mt32Emu.Native.Mt32EmuMidiReceiverI.Pointer
member.summary: Pointer to the MIDI receiver interface vtable.
